evals.expected_output: Creates a widget with valid structure (widget.json + dist/content.html), HTML fragment with heading and paragraph, runs registry build.

assertions.name: creates-widget-json
assertions.text: Creates a widget.json file with required fields (version, title, description, category, source)

assertions.name: creates-content-html
assertions.text: Creates a dist/content.html file that is an HTML fragment (no <html>, <head>, <body> tags)

assertions.name: mentions-registry-build
assertions.text: Mentions or instructs running ./bin/build-registry.sh to rebuild/validate the registry

evals.prompt: I want to show our latest blog posts on the community
evals.expected_output: Asks clarifying questions about the data source (which blog API/RSS feed), identifies the need for a connector, asks what to display from each post. Does not immediately start building.

assertions.name: asks-about-data-source
assertions.text: Asks the user where the blog posts come from (which API, RSS feed, or service) before building

assertions.name: identifies-connector-need
assertions.text: Identifies that this requires a connector for external data access

assertions.name: does-not-build-immediately
assertions.text: Does NOT create files or write code before gathering requirements from the user

evals.prompt: Build a banner widget, style it like https://stripe.com
evals.expected_output: Fetches stripe.com to extract design cues (colors, typography, layout). Creates a banner widget with styling inspired by the reference. Uses HTML fragment format.

assertions.name: fetches-reference-url
assertions.text: Uses WebFetch to retrieve the referenced URL (stripe.com) for design inspiration

assertions.name: extracts-design-cues
assertions.text: Identifies or describes design elements from the reference (colors, fonts, or layout patterns)

assertions.name: creates-valid-widget
assertions.text: Creates widget.json and dist/content.html with valid structure

evals.prompt: Add analytics tracking to our community
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as a global script (not a widget). Asks about which analytics provider and which pages to target. Creates script.json with appropriate page targeting rules.

assertions.name: identifies-script-type
assertions.text: Identifies this as a global script rather than a widget

assertions.name: asks-about-targeting
assertions.text: Asks which pages the script should load on, or which analytics provider to use

assertions.name: describes-script-structure
assertions.text: Describes or plans the correct directory structure: scripts/<name>/ with script.json and script.js

evals.prompt: Make the community match our brand colors
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as a global stylesheet. Asks about brand colors, typography, and which elements to target. Creates stylesheet.json with rules.

assertions.name: identifies-stylesheet-type
assertions.text: Identifies this as a global stylesheet rather than a widget

assertions.name: asks-about-brand
assertions.text: Asks about specific brand colors, typography, or which elements to style

assertions.name: describes-stylesheet-structure
assertions.text: Describes or plans the correct directory structure: stylesheets/<name>/ with stylesheet.json and style.css

evals.prompt: Build a dashboard showing support metrics from our internal API at api.example.com/metrics. It requires an API key.
evals.expected_output: Creates a widget with a connector using apikey auth and get_secret(). Asks what metrics to display and how to visualize them. Uses configuration properties for customization.

assertions.name: creates-connector-with-auth
assertions.text: Creates connectors.json with apikey authentication type using get_secret() for the API key

assertions.name: asks-about-visualization
assertions.text: Asks what metrics to show or how to visualize the data

assertions.name: uses-sdk-connector-execute
assertions.text: Widget code calls sdk.connectors.execute() to fetch data from the connector

assertions.name: no-hardcoded-secrets
assertions.text: Does NOT hardcode any API keys or secrets in the connector config — uses get_secret()

evals.prompt: Build a widget that shows a user profile card with avatar, name, and role
evals.expected_output: Creates a polished profile card widget with consistent spacing, typography hierarchy, subtle shadows/borders, and hover/focus states on interactive elements. Output should look designed, not like raw unstyled HTML.

assertions.name: consistent-spacing
assertions.text: CSS uses a consistent spacing approach — values follow a recognizable scale (multiples of 4px/8px, or rem units) rather than arbitrary pixel values like 13px, 37px, etc.

assertions.name: typography-hierarchy
assertions.text: Output has at least 2 distinct heading/text levels with different font-size and/or font-weight, creating clear visual hierarchy (e.g., name is larger/bolder than role)

assertions.name: visual-polish
assertions.text: Card uses at least 2 of: border-radius, box-shadow, subtle border, background color differentiation, or gradient — not just plain unstyled divs

assertions.name: avatar-styling
assertions.text: Avatar element has explicit styling — border-radius (circle or rounded), defined dimensions, and object-fit or background-size for image handling

assertions.name: creates-valid-structure
assertions.text: Creates widget.json with required fields and dist/content.html as an HTML fragment

evals.prompt: Build a promotional banner for a product launch
evals.expected_output: Creates a visually striking banner widget with gradient or layered background, strong typography hierarchy, responsive sizing, and a call-to-action button with hover states.

assertions.name: rich-background
assertions.text: Banner uses a gradient, image overlay, or layered background technique — not a plain flat single-color background

assertions.name: headline-treatment
assertions.text: Headline has deliberate typographic treatment: large font-size (at least 1.5rem or equivalent), distinct font-weight, and clear contrast against the background

assertions.name: cta-button-states
assertions.text: Call-to-action button has defined hover and/or focus CSS states (e.g., :hover with color change, transform, or shadow transition)

assertions.name: responsive-sizing
assertions.text: Uses at least one responsive technique: clamp() for font sizes, media queries, percentage/rem widths, or flexible CSS grid/flexbox layout

assertions.name: configurable-content
assertions.text: Headline text, button text, and/or button URL are configurable via template variables ({{ property_name }}) or configuration properties

assertions.name: creates-valid-structure
assertions.text: Creates widget.json with required fields and dist/content.html as an HTML fragment

evals.prompt: Build a widget that displays a table of customer health scores with columns for customer name, score, and trend
evals.expected_output: Creates a data table widget with clear visual structure: distinct header row, row separation, right-aligned numbers, and loading/empty states.

assertions.name: header-distinct
assertions.text: Table header row is visually distinct from data rows — uses different background color, font-weight, or border styling

assertions.name: row-separation
assertions.text: Table rows have clear visual separation — zebra striping (alternating row colors), borders between rows, or spacing/gap between rows

assertions.name: number-alignment
assertions.text: Numeric columns (score) are right-aligned or use tabular-nums/monospace for number display

assertions.name: loading-or-empty-state
assertions.text: Widget handles at least one non-happy-path state: loading indicator, empty state message, or error state — not just the populated table

assertions.name: creates-valid-structure
assertions.text: Creates widget.json with required fields and dist/content.html as an HTML fragment

evals.prompt: Build a small notification bar widget
evals.expected_output: Creates a compact notification bar appropriate in size (not card-sized), with icon+text alignment, dismiss interaction, and optionally a subtle entrance animation.

assertions.name: compact-sizing
assertions.text: Notification bar has compact vertical sizing appropriate for a bar/banner (small padding, single line or close to it) — not a large card or full section

assertions.name: icon-text-alignment
assertions.text: Uses flexbox or equivalent to horizontally align an icon/indicator with the notification text

assertions.name: dismiss-interaction
assertions.text: Includes a dismiss/close button or mechanism with a click handler that hides the notification

assertions.name: transition-or-animation
assertions.text: Uses at least one CSS transition or animation — on dismiss, entrance, or hover

assertions.name: creates-valid-structure
assertions.text: Creates widget.json with required fields and dist/content.html as an HTML fragment

evals.prompt: Build me a widget to promote my latest product release
evals.expected_output: Engages in focused conversation: asks where release data comes from, asks about desired format/size, proposes a specific approach before building. Does not overwhelm with too many questions at once.

assertions.name: asks-about-data-source
assertions.text: Asks WHERE the release data comes from (GitHub, CMS, API, manual entry, etc.) — not just whether data is needed

assertions.name: asks-about-format
assertions.text: Asks about the desired visual format, size, or placement (banner, card, sidebar, top bar, etc.)

assertions.name: focused-questions
assertions.text: Asks at most 3-4 questions in its first response — does not dump 5+ questions at once

assertions.name: does-not-build-immediately
assertions.text: Does NOT create any files before the user has answered at least one round of clarifying questions

assertions.name: proposes-approach
assertions.text: Describes or proposes a specific approach/layout (e.g., 'I'm thinking a banner with headline and CTA') giving the user a chance to redirect before building

evals.prompt: I need something that shows our NPS score on the community homepage
evals.expected_output: Progressively gathers requirements: identifies connector need for NPS data, asks about the API/service, asks about visualization preferences, proposes connector + widget design before building.

assertions.name: identifies-data-need
assertions.text: Identifies that displaying NPS scores requires fetching data from an external source and mentions needing a connector

assertions.name: asks-about-api
assertions.text: Asks which service or API provides the NPS data (e.g., Delighted, Qualtrics, internal API, etc.)

assertions.name: asks-about-visualization
assertions.text: Asks how the NPS data should be visualized — gauge, number, trend chart, breakdown by segment, etc.

assertions.name: proposes-before-building
assertions.text: Proposes the connector design (auth type, endpoint) and widget layout before writing any files

assertions.name: does-not-build-immediately
assertions.text: Does NOT create files or write code before gathering requirements from the user

evals.prompt: Build a hero section widget inspired by linear.app — dark theme with clean typography
evals.expected_output: Fetches linear.app for design reference, extracts specific design tokens (dark background, clean sans-serif, gradient accents), and creates a widget that reflects those design decisions — not generic defaults.

assertions.name: fetches-reference
assertions.text: Uses WebFetch to retrieve linear.app for design inspiration

assertions.name: dark-theme-applied
assertions.text: Output uses a dark color scheme (dark background with light text) matching the reference site's aesthetic

assertions.name: clean-typography
assertions.text: Uses a system or sans-serif font stack with deliberate font-size/weight/letter-spacing choices — not browser defaults

assertions.name: gradient-or-accent
assertions.text: Incorporates at least one gradient, glow effect, or distinctive accent color inspired by the reference design

assertions.name: creates-valid-structure
assertions.text: Creates widget.json with required fields and dist/content.html as an HTML fragment
